metadata.title: Claude, Judged
metadata.color_semantics: claude: INK #3D3929 all text; SPARK #D97757 the one accent; callout ink #161511 on cream; code stays Onda dark glass
metadata.note: COLD OPEN LAW: B00 = ClaudeComposerAsk. SPARK-LINE LAW: inner beats pair the spark with one short serif line; typing is B00-only. OUTRO LAW: outro restates the title. Season finale — recaps all seven prior episodes. Spine: B00 ASK · B01 GATE-P (real code from generate_audio.py) · B02 DETERMINABLE-VS-JUDGEABLE · B03 GATES-CHEAP · B04 AGENTS-CHECK · B05 NEVER-DELEGATES · B06 SEASON-RECAP · B07 VERDICT (artifact: who checks what) · B08 title-restate outro.
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

beats.narration_text: Let's recap with Claude. Before any audio spend, GATE P confirms the pedagogy file contains one line: VERDICT: PASS. Determinable checks like schema, beat count, and file paths are answered cheaply and correctly by agents. Judgeable questions about interest, pacing, and worth publishing stay with humans watching the actual cut. The agent arrives maximally informed but minimally autonomous, bringing everything it knows without making the human decision.

props.artifactTitle: GATE P reads a line, not the video

Judgeable questions about interest, pacing, and worth publishing stay with humans watching the actual cut.
The agent arrives maximally informed but minimally autonomous, bringing everything it knows without making the human decision.

beats.narration_text: GATE P reading a single VERDICT: PASS line before any audio spend is the exact architecture I want to copy. I'll map my own workflow's checks and try to place the cheap gate right before its most expensive step. Can you help me (1) list which of my checks are determinable enough to become GATE-P-style text reads, (2) identify the one truly expensive taste-check that must stay human, and (3) describe how 'maximally informed, minimally autonomous' would shape what the agent hands me at that moment?

props.command: GATE P reading a single VERDICT: PASS line before any audio spend is the exact architecture I want to copy. beats.narration_text: GATE P. Before any audio spend, the pedagogy file must contain one line: VERDICT: PASS. The gate does not watch the video. It reads a file. That is the entire job — and it is exactly the right job. Cheap, deterministic, correct.

props.title: GATE P — generate_audio.py
props.code:
ped = Path(slug_dir) / 'PEDAGOGY.md'
if not re.search(
    r'^\s*VERDICT:\s*PASS\b', ped.read_text()
):
    print('[err] GATE P: no VERDICT: PASS')
    sys.exit(3)
# cheap. deterministic. correct.

beats.narration_text: Two kinds of questions. Determinable: does the schema validate? Does the timing file have nine beats? Does the mp4 exist at the expected path? Agents answer these correctly and cheaply. Judgeable: is this interesting? Is the pacing right? Is it worth publishing? No agent answers these. Humans do.

Gates are cheap taste-checks on the plan before the expensive taste-check on the video. GATE P stops the audio spend if the pedagogy audit fails — not because it watched the video, but because it read one line of text. That is the right architecture. The expensive gate — watching the actual cut — is the human.

What agents check: schema valid. Beat count matches. Duration within bounds. MP4 exists, correct resolution, correct length. QC stills generated. These are all determinable. An agent checking these is doing its correct and full job — not falling short, not exceeding its scope.

What never delegates: whether this is good. Whether it earns the viewer's time. Whether the pacing serves the idea. Whether to publish. Maximally informed, minimally autonomous. The agent brings everything it knows to the human decision. It does not make the human decision.

The whole series in one pass. On empty: routed models by tier. Unsupervised: scoped the risk before expanding the trust. Taught: froze the judgment into a skill so runners never improvise. Scripted: pinned the exact so it never re-derives. On average: named what Claude is — a distribution. Steered: loaded the dice with conditioning. And judged: who signs. That is the system.

Who checks what. Agents check the determinable: schema, timing, paths, resolution. Humans check the judgeable: interesting, worth watching, publish. Gates are cheap taste-checks before expensive ones. Maximally informed, minimally autonomous. The determinism boundary is the human-judgment boundary. The whole system is a careful answer to one question: what can be checked — and what has to be decided.

artifactLines: Agents check the determinable: schema, timing, paths, resolution.
Humans check the judgeable: interesting, worth watching, publish.
Gates are cheap taste-checks before expensive ones.
The determinism boundary is the human-judgment boundary.
